key_insight: PriEco-DRL jointly coordinates electric-bus eco-driving and transit-priority adaptive signals to cut energy use while preserving punctuality, comfort, and network efficiency.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical control strategy for real-time transit corridors, helping operators tune the energy–time trade-off without sacrificing bus priority.
method: Priority-weighted max-pressure signal control allocates green time using occupancy-aware pressures.
A DRL vehicle controller learns local eco-driving actions from uncertain, evolving signal cues.
Centralized training with decentralized execution and parameter sharing lets one agent generalize across buses and routes.
evidence_signal: On a real-world corridor, the method was evaluated against fixed-time, actuated, and rule-based baselines and reported lower energy consumption with maintained transit priority and network performance.
watch_item: Performance depends on reward weighting and reliable local signal observations, so deployment may require careful calibration and robustness checks under noisy communication or atypical traffic conditions.
paper_title: PriEco-DRL: Joint Optimization of Electric-Bus Eco-Driving and Transit-Priority Adaptive Signals via Deep Reinforcement Learning
